methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $360M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in Space & Aerospace. Range: $1.0B - $2.5B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 64.2x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
K2 Space is a Series C-stage space and aerospace company founded by Karan and Neel Kunjur that has raised $360M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws from a peer universe of 5 public comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private companies in the same sector and stage, including Axiom Space ($2.5B), Impulse Space ($1.8B), Varda Space Industries ($1.5B), Skyryse ($1.1B), and Isar Aerospace ($1.0B).

Two triangulation methods prove applicable. The stage-progression approach applies a 1.7x step-up multiplier to the $360M total raised proxy, yielding $612M. The peer-median method anchors to the five same-stage, same-sector private comparables, which show a median valuation of $1.5B with a range of $1.0B to $2.5B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied due to the absence of K2 Space's revenue data, a standard limitation for private company valuation.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $520M to $1.7B, with a median of $1.1B across the two applicable methods. The substantial spread reflects meaningful dispersion within the peer set and methodological differences. Key uncertainties include K2 Space's specific operational metrics, revenue trajectory, and technical differentiation relative to peers—factors that would tighten the range but remain unavailable in this dataset.
